NAME : DATE : 31/03/2026
AGE : 73 YRS. SEX : FEMALE
CATH ID : 2414/03/2026 IP NO : AHH-1798/03/26
CORONARY ANGIOPLASTY REPORT ( PTCA )
Route : LEFT FEMORAL ARTERY
Procedure : PTCA TO LCX
Access : 7F SHEATH
RFA : 130/80 mmhg
Heparin : 10,000 IU.
Guiding Catheter : 7F EBU 3.0
Coronary wire : Fielder FC Wire
Pre-dilatation : Sapphire 2.0 X 10 mm balloon at 16 atm.
Stent : LAD- GRACE 3.0 X 16 mm at 12 atm.[DES]
Post- dilatation : Sapphire nc 3.25 x 12 mm balloon at 19 atm.
Procedure : PTCA TO RCA
Access : 7F SHEATH
RFA/RRA : 130/80 mmhg
Guiding Catheter : 6F JR 3.0
Inj Heparin : 10000 IU
Coronary wire : Sion Blue
Pre-dilatation : Sapphire 2.0 X 10 mm balloon at 16 atm.
Stent : RCA- GRACE 2.75 X 32 mm at 11 atm.[DES]
Post- dilatation : Sapphire nc 3.0 x 10 mm balloon at 19 atm.
POST PTCA ACT : 290
Final result : Excellent final result , no haze / dissection / thrombus
TIMI III Flow
Residual stenosis 0%

सुचना * पुडील केलेस येताना हा कामद सोबत आणावा. * औषधी अंतर्ज्ञान असल्यास प्रथम सांगणे. * औषधे डॉक्टरांना दाखवूच घ्या.

records.summary: This is a typed PTCA (Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty) report detailing the procedure performed on a 73-year-old female patient on March 31, 2026. The report describes interventions on both the left circumflex (LCX) and right coronary artery (RCA) using stents and balloons, concluding with an excellent result, TIMI III flow, and 0% residual stenosis.
key_observations: Procedure performed on March 31, 2026, on a 73-year-old female.
PTCA performed on LCX and RCA arteries.
Stents deployed: LAD-GRACE 3.0 X 16 mm and RCA-GRACE 2.75 X 32 mm.
Final result: Excellent, with TIMI III flow and 0% residual stenosis.
Patient was given 10,000 IU of heparin during the procedure.

records.body_region: Coronary tree — left system, Right coronary artery
records.stage_evidence: PTCA TO LCX & RCA WITH 2 STENT DONE

ocr.verbatim:
LCX : Using 7F EBU 3.0 catheter,Left coronary ostium was engaged and 0.014 Fielder FC Wire was advanced in the distal LAD.The lesion was dilated with Sapphire 2.0 X 10 mm balloon at 16 atm,and stented with LAD- GRACE 3.0 X 16 mm at 12 atm,Post-dilatation done with Sapphire nc 3.25 x 12 mm balloon at 19atm.with an excellent final result. .

RCA : Using 6F JR 3.0 catheter ,Right coronary ostium was engaged and 0.014 Sion Blue wire was advanced in the distal RCA.The lesion was dilated with Sapphire 2.0 X 10 mm balloon at 16 atm, and stented with GRACE 2.75 X 32 mm at 11 atm. Post-dilatation done with Sapphire nc 3.0 x 10 mm balloon at 19 atm. with an excellent final result.

PTCA TO LCX & RCA WITH 2 STENT DONE.
LONG TERM DUAL ANTIPLATELETS.
Note: All stents having inherent risk of restenosis and thrombosis.Please continue antiplatelet drugs regularly. Do not stop medicines without consulting Physician or Cardiologist

सुचना * पुड़ील वेक्स येतांना हा कमाद सोबत आपणावा. * औषधाची अंतर्ल्यास प्रथम सांगणे. * औषधे डॉक्टरांना वाखूरूच घ्या.
Phone :

RCA : Using 6F JR 3.0 catheter ,Right coronary ostium was engaged and 0.014 Sion Blue wire was advanced in the distal RCA.The lesion was dilated with Sapphire 2.0 X 10 mm balloon at 16 atm, and stented with GRACE 2.75 X 32 mm at 11 atm. Post-dilatation done with Sapphire nc 3.0 x 10 mm balloon at 19 atm. with an excellent final result.

records.summary: This is a typed procedural report detailing a percutaneous coronary intervention (PTCA) performed on the left circumflex artery (LCX) and right coronary artery (RCA), including balloon angioplasty and stent placement. The procedure was completed successfully with an excellent final result. The report also includes a note advising long-term dual antiplatelet therapy and warning about the inherent risks of stents.
key_observations: PTCA performed on LCX and RCA with 2 stents deployed
LCX: Stented with LAD-GRACE 3.0 X 16 mm at 12 atm; post-dilatation with Sapphire nc 3.25 x 12 mm balloon at 19 atm
RCA: Stented with GRACE 2.75 X 32 mm at 11 atm; post-dilatation with Sapphire nc 3.0 x 10 mm balloon at 19 atm
Patient advised to continue long-term dual antiplatelet therapy
Note on inherent risks of stents: restenosis and thrombosis

records.stage_evidence: Stents visible in RCA and LCX branches

records.summary: This is a typed Coronary Angiography Report for a 73-year-old female patient. The report details findings of stenosis in the left circumflex and right coronary arteries, with an impression of 'DVD' (Disease of the Vessels) and a recommendation for PTCA (Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty) on the LCX and RCA.
key_observations: Patient is 73 years old, female.
Left Main and LAD arteries are described as normal.
Left Circumflex artery shows 95% stenosis at the ostium with a mid-plaque.
Right Coronary Artery shows 80% stenosis at the ostium.
Recommendation is PTCA for LCX and RCA.
Report includes a notice in Hindi regarding document and allergy information.

records.stage_evidence: Handwritten intervention plan (PTCA) visible
